I agree because the NESTROFT test specifically detects IgG antibodies on red blood cells, which is characteristic of autoimmune hemolytic anemia, unlike other options like hereditary spherocytosis (which involves structural defects), β-thalassemia (a genetic disorder affecting heme synthesis), or megaloblastic anemia (related to vitamin B12/folate deficiency). 

Answer Choice: C
